Package com.saperion.jcs.modules.acl.connector

Class AccessManagement

java.lang.Object
com.saperion.jcs.modules.acl.connector.AccessManagement
public final class AccessManagement extends Object
The AccessManagement provides methods to search for, create, update and delete ACLs. To use the AccessManagement, the saperion.properties of the Java Core Server must include the line rmi.accessservice.enabled=true.

To create a new instance, use

AccessManagement accessManagement = new AccessManagement.Config(connector).build();

where connector is an initialized and logged-on instance of the SaClassicConnector.

    • init

      protected void init() throws RemoteException, NotBoundException

    • create

      public com.saperion.jcs.modules.acl.api.ACL create(com.saperion.jcs.modules.acl.api.ACL acl) throws AuthenticationException, BackendException
      Creates a new ACL.
      Parameters:
      acl - the ACL to create
      Returns:
      the created ACL
      Throws:
      AuthenticationException - not logged on or insufficient rights
      BackendException - error when creating the new ACL

    • update

      public com.saperion.jcs.modules.acl.api.ACL update(com.saperion.jcs.modules.acl.api.ACL acl) throws AuthenticationException, BackendException
      Updates an existing ACL.
      Parameters:
      acl - the ACL with updated values
      Returns:
      the updated ACL
      Throws:
      AuthenticationException - not logged on or insufficient rights
      BackendException - error when updating the ACL

    • delete

      public void delete(String name) throws AuthenticationException, BackendException
      Deletes an existing ACL.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the ACL to delete
      Throws:
      AuthenticationException - not logged on or insufficient rights
      BackendException - error when deleting the ACL

    • find

      public com.saperion.jcs.modules.acl.api.ACL find(String name) throws AuthenticationException, BackendException
      Finds the ACL with the specified name.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the ACL to find
      Returns:
      the ACL with the specified name or null if no ACL was found
      Throws:
      AuthenticationException - not logged on or insufficient rights
      BackendException - error when searching for the ACL
